subject: Pharmacy deal flow — from an operator, not a banker
plain:
Hi Ross Kowalsky,

I'm Parthiv Shah — registered pharmacist, owner of 21 pharmacy locations, and Chairman of the Pharmacist Society of New York. I run rxbuysell.com, a sourcing platform built specifically for pharmacy M&A. Most pharmacy deals that reach Mckesson come through generalist brokers who don't understand DIR fees, PBM contracts, or script-volume seasonality. We do.

What we bring: off-market and pre-marketed independent pharmacy listings (retail, specialty, LTC), operator-vetted financials with real add-back commentary, and an active pipeline across NY, NJ, CT, and expanding nationally. We're currently running a 5-site Connecticut portfolio ($49.6MM revenue) with more in quiet marketing.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if there's a fit?

Parthiv Shah, RPh
Owner, DMS Pharmacy Group | rxbuysell.com
(908) 224-7624 | parthiv@rxbuysell.com | rxbuysell.com

—
You're receiving this because pharmacy M&A may be relevant to your firm. To stop receiving these emails, reply with 'unsubscribe'.

We have an off-market Connecticut retail pharmacy portfolio currently in diligence: five operating locations across Bridgeport, New Haven, and Waterbury, $49.6MM FY2025 revenue and $3.0MM Adjusted EBITDA, available as a full portfolio or in subgroups on a cash-free, debt-free basis.
